question:
Implement a function to find the minimum time required to finish all tasks in a circular manner. You are given an integer array task where task[i] represents the time taken to complete the ith task. Return the minimum time required to finish all tasks. You should also find the minimum time at which the tasks can be completed such that the order of completing tasks is not the same as the order in which they were given.

Ask for time and space complexity of your solution.

The array can contain duplicate task times, but the tasks can be performed in a circular manner. 

Example: input [1,2,3] or [5,3,5]

Here's your task: Given an array of integers, find the first duplicate in the array. The integers are between 1 and n where n is the size of the array, and no integer is greater than n. If no duplicate exists return -1. Can you do this in O(n) time and O(1) space complexity, assuming you cannot use any additional data structures?
